cues.label: High-level map
cues.headline: Hidden state becomes a next-token guess
cues.narration: A transformer mixes information across the prompt. The output head turns the final hidden state into logits — one score per possible next token. Softmax makes them probabilities, generation picks one, appends it, and repeats.

cues.label: Analogy
cues.headline: The output head is a scoring station
cues.narration: Picture the pipeline as a workshop line. The transformer mixes context, the output-head station scores every word in the vocabulary, softmax stamps a receipt of probabilities, and generation picks one token to send forward.

cues.label: Tiny example
cues.headline: Follow one concrete prompt
cues.narration: Take one small prompt and follow it. In 'The cat', the final position's hidden state summarizes everything read so far. That single vector is what the model turns into a guess about the very next word.

cues.label: Mechanism
cues.headline: Logits are the hidden state scored per word
cues.narration: The output head is a matrix with one row per vocabulary word. Multiplying it by the hidden state gives a logit for each word — a raw, unbounded score. Here 'sat' scores highest, so it will win once we normalize.

cues.label: Implementation
cues.headline: Softmax turns scores into probabilities
cues.narration: Softmax exponentiates every logit and divides by their sum, so the scores become positive numbers that add to one. In code: take the logit vector, apply softmax, and read off P('sat'). Argmax picks the top token; sampling can pick others.

cues.label: Misconception
cues.headline: A score is not yet a probability
cues.narration: Separate nearby ideas: a logit is not a probability until softmax, a high probability is not certainty, argmax is not the only way to choose, and naming a phase like 'predict' is not the same as the matrix multiply inside it.

cues.label: Synthesis
cues.headline: Sample, append, and predict again
cues.narration: Return to the route: hidden state, logits, softmax, choose a token. Then append it and run the whole loop again — that autoregressive loop is how context grows and how each new word changes the next prediction. The audio maps it, the visual shows it, the code proves it.
